normal.sentence: It means repeated practice can slowly change what feels normal.

temperament.contextMeaning: A person's natural style, like calm or active

person's.sentence: Temperament means a person's natural style, such as being more calm, more sensitive, or more active.

style.contextMeaning: The way a person usually is

all.sentence: Social environment matters because family, school, work, money, and culture all shape behavior.

matter.sentence: Values matter because people sometimes choose a difficult action on purpose, even before it becomes a habit.

sometimes.contextMeaning: At some times, but not always

idea.sentence: How Change Starts A useful practical idea is identity-based change.

instead.sentence: Instead of only saying, 'I want better results,' a person repeats a small action that supports a new self-description.

reading.contextMeaning: Spending time with a book or text
reading.sentence: Reading five pages each night can support, 'I am a reader.' Walking after lunch can support, 'I am an active person.' The habit is small, but the repeated action becomes evidence.

over.sentence: Over time, that evidence can make a new identity feel more believable.

make.simpleMeaning: Cause something to happen or be
make.contextMeaning: Cause the new identity to feel more real
make.sentence: Over time, that evidence can make a new identity feel more believable.

believable.contextMeaning: Easy to feel true in your mind

balanced.contextMeaning: Showing both sides, not only habits
balanced.sentence: A Balanced Answer So, is personality just a collection of habits?

includes.contextMeaning: Has traits, temperament, values, and social context as parts
includes.sentence: Personality also includes traits, temperament, values, and social context.

context.simpleMeaning: The things around something that help explain it
context.contextMeaning: The situation and things around behavior that help explain it
context.sentence: Personality also includes traits, temperament, values, and social context.

turn.simpleMeaning: To make something become something else
turn.contextMeaning: Make repeated behavior become proof every day
turn.sentence: But habits still matter because they turn repeated behavior into daily evidence.

daily.contextMeaning: Used in normal life each day
daily.sentence: But habits still matter because they turn repeated behavior into daily evidence.

push.simpleMeaning: To move something forward or make it go
push.contextMeaning: Make identity move in one clear way
push.sentence: Over time, that evidence can push identity in a clear direction.

direction.sentence: Over time, that evidence can push identity in a clear direction.

help.simpleMeaning: To make something easier or better
help.contextMeaning: Make it easier to grow the person people see
help.sentence: Habits are not the whole person, but they can help build the person other people see and the person we think we are.

build.simpleMeaning: To make something little by little
build.contextMeaning: Make the person people see, little by little
build.sentence: Habits are not the whole person, but they can help build the person other people see and the person we think we are.

see.contextMeaning: Notice the person other people notice
see.sentence: Habits are not the whole person, but they can help build the person other people see and the person we think we are.

think.simpleMeaning: To use your mind to know or believe something
think.contextMeaning: Believe about ourselves in our minds
think.sentence: Habits are not the whole person, but they can help build the person other people see and the person we think we are.

stone.sentence: Personality is not a fixed stone, and it is not only a pile of habits either.

pile.contextMeaning: A big group of habits together
pile.sentence: Personality is not a fixed stone, and it is not only a pile of habits either.

either.simpleMeaning: Used to show another negative idea too
either.contextMeaning: Also not just a group of habits
either.sentence: Personality is not a fixed stone, and it is not only a pile of habits either.

together.simpleMeaning: With each other, as one group
together.sentence: A better answer is that stable traits and repeated actions work together.

understand.contextMeaning: Know what the cue, routine, reward, and situation mean
understand.sentence: If you understand the cue, the routine, the reward, and the wider context around behavior, change becomes easier to explain.

wider.sentence: If you understand the cue, the routine, the reward, and the wider context around behavior, change becomes easier to explain.

around.simpleMeaning: Near something; on all sides of it
around.contextMeaning: About the things near behavior and connected to it
around.sentence: If you understand the cue, the routine, the reward, and the wider context around behavior, change becomes easier to explain.

life.simpleMeaning: The time when you are alive
life.contextMeaning: Everyday living and normal daily activities
life.sentence: That is useful in psychology, and it is useful in daily life.

minor.sentence: One small action may look minor, but repeated actions can slowly change what feels natural and who you believe you are becoming.

who.sentence: One small action may look minor, but repeated actions can slowly change what feels natural and who you believe you are becoming.

believe.contextMeaning: Think you are becoming a certain kind of person
believe.sentence: One small action may look minor, but repeated actions can slowly change what feels natural and who you believe you are becoming.

becoming.sentence: One small action may look minor, but repeated actions can slowly change what feels natural and who you believe you are becoming.

news.simpleMeaning: New information about things that happened
news.contextMeaning: A report about something that happened
news.sentence: [NEWS STORY] Traits and Habits In psychology, a trait is a broad pattern in how a person usually thinks, feels, or acts across time and across situations.

story.simpleMeaning: Something told about people or events
story.contextMeaning: A report about a real event
story.sentence: [NEWS STORY] Traits and Habits In psychology, a trait is a broad pattern in how a person usually thinks, feels, or acts across time and across situations.

question.contextMeaning: A sentence asking you to think and answer
question.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: What is the difference between a trait and a habit?

difference.simpleMeaning: How two things are not the same
difference.contextMeaning: How a trait and a habit are not the same
difference.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: What is the difference between a trait and a habit?

between.simpleMeaning: In the middle of two things
between.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: What is the difference between a trait and a habit?

while.simpleMeaning: At the same time as something else
while.contextMeaning: Showing two ideas in one sentence
while.sentence: ANSWER: A trait is a broad pattern in personality, while a habit is a repeated action that becomes easier through repetition.

conscious.simpleMeaning: Awake and knowing what you do
conscious.sentence: ANSWER: It means an action needs less conscious effort because it has been repeated many times.

deliberate.simpleMeaning: Done on purpose, not by accident
deliberate.sentence: ANSWER: Because temperament, social environment, values, and deliberate choices also shape behavior and identity.

choices.sentence: ANSWER: Because temperament, social environment, values, and deliberate choices also shape behavior and identity.

affect.simpleMeaning: To change something or make it different
affect.sentence: QUESTION: How can a small repeated action affect identity?

creates.simpleMeaning: Makes something new happen or exist
creates.contextMeaning: Makes daily proof that a new self-story is true
creates.sentence: ANSWER: It creates daily evidence that supports a new self-description, such as seeing yourself as organized or active.

seeing.simpleMeaning: Looking at something with your eyes
seeing.contextMeaning: Thinking of yourself as organized or active
seeing.sentence: ANSWER: It creates daily evidence that supports a new self-description, such as seeing yourself as organized or active.

closing.contextMeaning: The last part of the message
closing.sentence: [CLOSING] Personality is not a fixed stone, and it is not only a pile of habits either.
